## Select your cookie preferences

We use essential cookies and similar tools that are necessary to provide our site and services. We use performance cookies to collect anonymous statistics, so we can understand how customers use our site and make improvements. Essential cookies cannot be deactivated, but you can choose “Customize” or “Decline” to decline performance cookies.

If you agree, AWS and approved third parties will also use cookies to provide useful site features, remember your preferences, and display relevant content, including relevant advertising. To accept or decline all non-essential cookies, choose “Accept” or “Decline.” To make more detailed choices, choose “Customize.”

AcceptDeclineCustomize

## Customize cookie preferences

We use cookies and similar tools (collectively, "cookies") for the following purposes.

### Essential

Essential cookies are necessary to provide our site and services and cannot be deactivated. They are usually set in response to your actions on the site, such as setting your privacy preferences, signing in, or filling in forms.

### Performance

Performance cookies provide anonymous statistics about how customers navigate our site so we can improve site experience and performance. Approved third parties may perform analytics on our behalf, but they cannot use the data for their own purposes.

Allowed

### Functional

Functional cookies help us provide useful site features, remember your preferences, and display relevant content. Approved third parties may set these cookies to provide certain site features. If you do not allow these cookies, then some or all of these services may not function properly.

Allowed

### Advertising

Advertising cookies may be set through our site by us or our advertising partners and help us deliver relevant marketing content. If you do not allow these cookies, you will experience less relevant advertising.

Allowed

Blocking some types of cookies may impact your experience of our sites. You may review and change your choices at any time by selecting Cookie preferences in the footer of this site. We and selected third-parties use cookies or similar technologies as specified in the [AWS Cookie Notice](https://aws.amazon.com/legal/cookies/).

CancelSave preferences

## Unable to save cookie preferences

We will only store essential cookies at this time, because we were unable to save your cookie preferences.

If you want to change your cookie preferences, try again later using the link in the AWS console footer, or contact support if the problem persists.

# What is Amazon Q Developer?

[PDF](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/pdfs/amazonq/latest/qdeveloper-ug/amazonq-developer-ug.pdf#what-is)

[RSS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazonq/latest/qdeveloper-ug/amazonq-developer-ug-updates.rss)

[Markdown](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazonq/latest/qdeveloper-ug/what-is.md "Download Markdown")

Focus mode

What is Amazon Q Developer? - Amazon Q Developer

[Open PDF](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/pdfs/amazonq/latest/qdeveloper-ug/amazonq-developer-ug.pdf#what-is "Open PDF")

[Get started](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazonq/latest/qdeveloper-ug/what-is.html#quick-start) [Amazon Q Developer pricing](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazonq/latest/qdeveloper-ug/what-is.html#pricing)

###### Note

Powered by Amazon Bedrock: Amazon Q Developer is built on Amazon Bedrock and includes [automated abuse\\
detection](https://docs.aws.amazon.com//bedrock/latest/userguide/abuse-detection.html) implemented in Amazon Bedrock to enforce safety, security, and the responsible use of AI.


Amazon Q Developer is a generative artificial intelligence (AI) powered conversational assistant
that can help you understand, build, extend, and operate AWS applications. You can ask
questions about AWS architecture, your AWS resources, best practices, documentation,
support, and more. Amazon Q is constantly updating its capabilities so your questions get the
most contextually relevant and actionable answers.

When used in an integrated development environment (IDE), Amazon Q provides software
development assistance. Amazon Q can chat about code, provide inline code completions,
generate net new code, scan your code for security vulnerabilities, and make code upgrades and
improvements, such as language updates, debugging, and optimizations.

Amazon Q is powered by [Amazon Bedrock](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/what-is-service.html), a fully managed service that makes foundation models (FMs) available through
an API. The model that powers Amazon Q has been augmented with high quality AWS content to get
you more complete, actionable, and referenced answers to accelerate your building on
AWS.

###### Note

This is the documentation for Amazon Q Developer. If you are looking for
documentation for Amazon Q Business, see the [Amazon Q Business User\\
Guide](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazonq/latest/business-use-dg/what-is.html).

## Get started with Amazon Q Developer

To quickly get started using Amazon Q, you can access it in the following ways:


**AWS apps and websites**

Add the
[necessary permissions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazonq/latest/qdeveloper-ug/security_iam_id-based-policy-examples.html) to your
IAM identity, and then choose the Amazon Q icon to start chatting in the AWS Management Console,
AWS Documentation website, AWS website, or AWS Console Mobile Application. For more information, see [Using Amazon Q Developer on AWS apps and websites](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazonq/latest/qdeveloper-ug/q-on-aws.html).

**IDEs**

Download the Amazon Q extension and use your AWS Builder ID (no AWS account
required) to sign in for free.

![Visual Studio Code logo, a stylized blue square with rounded corners and angled edges.](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/images/amazonq/latest/qdeveloper-ug/images/vs-code.png)[Download Amazon Q in Visual Studio Code](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=AmazonWebServices.amazon-q-vscode)

![The JetBrains logo.](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/images/amazonq/latest/qdeveloper-ug/images/jetbrains.png)[Download Amazon Q in JetBrains IDEs](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/plugin/24267-amazon-q/)

![The Visual Studio logo.](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/images/amazonq/latest/qdeveloper-ug/images/visual-studio.png)[Download Amazon Q in the AWS Toolkit for Visual Studio](https://marketplace.visualstudio.com/items?itemName=AmazonWebServices.AWSToolkitforVisualStudio2022)

![The Eclipse logo.](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/images/amazonq/latest/qdeveloper-ug/images/eclipse-logo.png)[Download Amazon Q in Eclipse IDEs (Preview)](https://marketplace.eclipse.org/content/amazon-q)

From your IDE, choose the Amazon Q icon to start
chatting or initiate a development workflow. For more information, see
[Installing the Amazon Q Developer extension or plugin in your IDE](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazonq/latest/qdeveloper-ug/q-in-IDE-setup.html).

**Amazon Q Developer in chat applications**

Add the [AmazonQDeveloperAccess](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazonq/latest/qdeveloper-ug/managed-policy.html#amazonq-policy-fullaccess) managed
policy to your IAM identity and channel guardrails for Microsoft
Teams or Slack applications. For more information, see [Chatting with Amazon Q Developer in chat applications](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazonq/latest/qdeveloper-ug/q-in-chat-applications.html).

## Amazon Q Developer pricing

Amazon Q Developer is available through a Free tier and the Amazon Q Developer Pro subscription. For
more information, see [Amazon Q Developer pricing](https://aws.amazon.com/q/developer/pricing).

We use cookies and similar tools (collectively, "cookies") for the following purposes.

### Essential

Essential cookies are necessary to provide our site and services and cannot be deactivated. They are usually set in response to your actions on the site, such as setting your privacy preferences, signing in, or filling in forms.

### Performance

Performance cookies provide anonymous statistics about how customers navigate our site so we can improve site experience and performance. Approved third parties may perform analytics on our behalf, but they cannot use the data for their own purposes.

Allowed

Functional cookies help us provide useful site features, remember your preferences, and display relevant content. Approved third parties may set these cookies to provide certain site features. If you do not allow these cookies, then some or all of these services may not function properly.

# The Complete Guide to Amazon Q CLI: Commands, Shortcuts, and Advanced Features

This guide covers all documented features of Amazon Q CLI as of September 2025. For the most up-to-date information, refer to the official GitHub repository and use the \`/changelog\` command to stay informed about new features and improvements.

![Omshree Butani](https://avatars.builderprofile.aws.dev/2eqnFkIPsqWdH22eq0i09Gl3ryt.webp)

[Omshree Butani](https://builder.aws.com/community/@omshree)

Follow

Community Builder

Published Sep 18, 2025

Last modified Feb 24, 2026

* * *

61

* * *

## **Introduction**

Amazon Q Developer CLI represents a paradigm shift in how developers interact with AI assistance. Amazon Q CLI integrates directly into your development workflow, providing contextual help, code assistance, and infrastructure management capabilities right from your terminal.

This comprehensive guide explores every command, shortcut, and feature available in Amazon Q CLI, helping you maximize productivity and streamline your development process.

* * *

## **What Makes Q CLI Special?**

- **Direct System Integration:** Execute bash commands, create apps, read/write files, and manage AWS resources
- **Context-Aware Assistance:** Understands your project structure, remembers your context and current working directory
- **Extensible Architecture:** Support for MCP servers and custom agents
- **Persistent Knowledge:** Maintain context across sessions with knowledge bases and conversation history

* * *

## **Core Commands**

### **Essential Navigation Commands**

1. **\`/quit\` - Exit Application**

- **Purpose**: Cleanly exit the Q CLI application
- **Use Case**: End your session when work is complete

1. **\`/clear\` - Reset Conversation**

- **Purpose**: Clear current conversation history while maintaining session
- **Use Case**: Start fresh on a new topic without losing configuration

1. **\`/help\` - Command Reference**

- **Purpose**: Display available commands and their usage
- **Use Case**: Quick reference when you need command syntax

### \-\-\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------      **Information and Status Commands**

1. **\`/usage\` - Context Window Status**

- **Purpose**: Display current session's context window utilization
- **Use Case**: Monitor token usage and optimize conversation length

1. **\`/tools\` - View Available Tools**

- **Purpose**: List all available tools and their permission status
- **Use Case**: Understand what capabilities are available in your current session

1. **\`/mcp\` - MCP Server Status**

- **Purpose**: Display loaded Model Context Protocol servers
- **Use Case**: Verify external tool integrations are working

1. **\`/changelog\` - Version Information**

- **Purpose**: View recent updates and changes to Q CLI
- **Use Case**: Stay informed about new features and improvements

### \-\-\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### **Conversation Management**

1. **\`/save\` - Preserve Conversations**

- **Usage**:
  - _\`/save\`_ \- Save with auto-generated name
  - _\`/save \[name\]\`_ \- Save with custom name
- **Purpose**: Store conversation history for later retrieval
- **Use Cases**:
  - Preserve important troubleshooting sessions
  - Save complex configuration discussions
  - Create reusable solution templates

1. **\`/load\` - Restore Conversations**

- **Purpose**: Interactive selection and restoration of saved conversations
- **Use Cases**:
  - Continue previous work sessions
  - Reference past solutions
  - Share conversation context with team members

#### \-\-\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------      **Conversation Optimization**

**\`/compact\` - Summarize Context**

- **Purpose**: Compress conversation history to free up context space
- **Use Cases**:
  - Maintain long-running sessions
  - Preserve key information while reducing token usage
  - Optimize performance in extended conversations

### \-\-\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------      **Agent System**

The agent system allows you to create specialized AI assistants with specific capabilities, tools, and knowledge bases.

#### **Agent Management Commands**

**\`/agent\` - Agent Operations**

- **Usage**:
  - \` _/agent\`_ \- List available agents
  - _\`/agent \[name\]\`_ \- Switch to specific agent
  - _\`/agent generate\`_ \- Create new agent with Q's assistance
  - _\`/agent edit \[name\]\`_ \- Modify existing agent configuration
- **Purpose**: Manage specialized AI assistants
- **Use Cases**:
  - Create project-specific assistants
  - Configure tool permissions and access
  - Maintain different contexts for different workflows

#### \-\-\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------      **Agent Configuration Locations**

**Local Agents** (Project-specific):

- .amazonq/cli-agents/

**Global Agents** (User-wide):

- ~/.aws/amazonq/cli-agents/

**Agent Configuration Example**

`{
"description": "AWS Infrastructure Specialist",
"tools": ["use_aws", "fs_read", "fs_write"],
"allowedTools": ["use_aws"],
"toolsSettings": {
"use_aws": {
"allowedServices": ["ec2", "s3", "lambda"],
"autoAllowReadonly": true
}
},
"resources": [\
"file://infrastructure/**/*.tf",\
"file://docs/aws-guidelines.md"\
]
}

`

### \-\-\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------    **Context Management**

1. **\`/context\` - Manage Context Files**

- **Usage**:
  - _\`/context\`_ \- List current context files
  - _\`/context add \[path\]\`_ \- Add files/directories to context
  - _\`/context remove \[path\]\`_ \- Remove from context
  - _\`/context clear\`_ \- Clear all context
- **Purpose**: Control what files and information Q can access
- **Use Cases**:
  - Provide project-specific context
  - Include relevant documentation
  - Control information scope for security

1. **\`/hooks\` - View Context Hooks**

- **Purpose**: Display active context hooks and their status
- **Use Case**: Understand what automatic context is being applied

### \-\-\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------      **Experimental Features**

Q CLI includes cutting-edge experimental features that can be enabled through the experiment system.

#### **Experiment Management**

**\`/experiment\` - Toggle Features**

- **Purpose**: Interactive menu to enable/disable experimental features
- **Available Experiments**:
  - _Knowledge_: Persistent context storage and semantic search
  - _Thinking_: Complex reasoning with visible thought processes
  - _Tangent Mode_: Conversation checkpointing for side discussions
  - _TODO Lists_: Automated task management and tracking

#### \-\-\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------      **Knowledge Base System**

When enabled, the knowledge experiment provides powerful context management:

**\`/knowledge\` - Knowledge Base Operations**

- **Usage**:
  - _\`/knowledge add \[path\]\`_ \- Add files/directories to knowledge base
  - _\`/knowledge show\`_ \- Display knowledge base contents
  - _\`/knowledge remove \[path\]\`_ \- Remove entries
  - _\`/knowledge update \[path\]\`_ \- Refresh knowledge base entries
  - _\`/knowledge clear\`_ \- Remove all entries
  - _\`/knowledge status\`_ \- Show background operation status
  - _\`/knowledge cancel\`_ \- Cancel background operations
- **Advanced Options**:
  - _\`--include \[pattern\]\`_ \- Include specific file patterns
  - _\`--exclude \[pattern\]\`_ \- Exclude file patterns
- **Use Cases**:
  - Maintain project-wide context across sessions
  - Enable semantic search across codebases
  - Provide persistent documentation access

#### \-\-\------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------      **Tangent Mode**

**\`/tangent\` - Conversation Checkpointing**

- **Usage**:
  - _\`/tangent\`_ \- Enter/exit tangent mode
  - _\`/tangent tail\`_ \- Exit tangent mode preserving last exchange
- **Purpose**: Explore side topics without disrupting main conversation
- **Use Cases**:
  - Ask clarifying questions
  - Explore alternative approaches
  - Get help with Q CLI features without losing context
- **Visual Indicators**:
  - Normal mode: \`> \` (magenta)
  - Tangent mode: \`↯ > \` (yellow ↯ + magenta)

#### \-\-\-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------      **TODO List Management**

**\`/todos\` - Task Management**

- **Usage**:
  - _\`/todos view\`_ \- Display TODO lists
  - _\`/todos resume\`_ \- Continue previous task list
  - _\`/todos clear-finished\`_ \- Remove completed lists
  - _\`/todos delete \[--all\]\`_ \- Delete specific or all lists
- **Purpose**: Manage complex multi-step tasks
- **Use Cases**:
  - Track project milestones
  - Resume interrupted workflows
  - Organize development tasks

* * *

## **Built-in Tools**

Q CLI includes powerful built-in tools that extend its capabilities. Let's explore few of them.

### **File System Tools**

#### **\`fs\_read\` - File Reading**

- **Capabilities**:
  - Read text files with line ranges
  - List directory contents with depth control
  - Search for patterns in files
  - Process images
- **Configuration Options**:

`{
"toolsSettings": {
"fs_read": {
"allowedPaths": ["~/projects", "./src/**"],
"deniedPaths": ["/sensitive/path/"]
}
}
}

`

#### **\`fs\_write\` - File Writing**

- **Capabilities**:
  - Create new files
  - Edit existing files with string replacement
  - Insert content at specific lines
  - Append to files
- **Configuration Options**:

`{
"toolsSettings": {
"fs_write": {
"allowedPaths": ["./output/**"],
"deniedPaths": ["/system/files/"]
}
}
}

`

#### **System Integration Tools**

**\`execute\_bash\` - Command Execution**

- **Capabilities**:
  - Execute shell commands
  - Run scripts and utilities
  - System administration tasks
- **Configuration Options**:

`{
"toolsSettings": {
"execute_bash": {
"allowedCommands": ["git status", "npm test"],
"deniedCommands": ["rm -rf .*"],
"autoAllowReadonly": true
}
}
}

`

#### **\`use\_aws\` - AWS Integration**

- **Capabilities**:
  - Make AWS CLI API calls
  - Manage AWS resources
  - Query AWS services
- **Configuration Options**:

`{
"toolsSettings": {
"use_aws": {
"allowedServices": ["s3", "lambda", "ec2"],
"deniedServices": ["iam"],
"autoAllowReadonly": true
}
}
}

`

#### **Utility Tools**

**\`introspect\` - Self-Awareness**

- **Purpose**: Provides Q CLI with knowledge about its own capabilities
- **Use Cases**:
  - Answer questions about Q CLI features
  - Provide command help and documentation
  - Explain available functionality

**\`report\_issue\` - Bug Reporting**

- **Purpose**: Opens GitHub issue template for reporting problems
- **Use Cases**:
  - Report bugs and issues
  - Request new features
  - Provide feedback

* * *

## **Configuration and Settings**

### **Settings Management**

Q CLI uses a comprehensive settings system managed through the terminal:

- View all settings: `q settings`
- Set specific values:

`q settings chat.enableTangentMode true`

`q settings chat.defaultModel claude-3-5-sonnet`
- View specific setting: `q settings chat.enableThinking`

### **Key Configuration Categories**

**Chat Settings**

- _\`chat.enableThinking\`_ \- Enable complex reasoning display
- _\`chat.enableTangentMode\`_ \- Enable conversation checkpointing
- _\`chat.tangentModeKey\`_ \- Keyboard shortcut for tangent mode
- _\`chat.defaultModel\`_ \- Default AI model selection
- _\`chat.defaultAgent\`_ \- Default agent configuration
- _\`chat.enableTodoList\`_ \- Enable TODO list functionality

**Knowledge Settings**

- _\`chat.enableKnowledge\`_ \- Enable knowledge base system
- _\`knowledge.maxFiles\`_ \- Maximum files for indexing
- _\`knowledge.chunkSize\`_ \- Text processing chunk size
- _\`knowledge.defaultIncludePatterns\`_ \- Default file inclusion patterns
- _\`knowledge.defaultExcludePatterns\`_ \- Default file exclusion patterns

**Interface Settings**

- _\`chat.skimCommandKey\`_ \- Fuzzy search keyboard shortcut
- _\`chat.disableMarkdownRendering\`_ \- Disable formatting
- _\`chat.enableNotifications\`_ \- Desktop notifications
- _\`chat.greeting.enabled\`_ \- Show startup greeting

**API Settings**

- _\`api.timeout\`_ \- Request timeout duration
- _\`api.codewhisperer.service\`_ \- CodeWhisperer endpoint
- _\`api.q.service\`_ \- Q service endpoint

* * *

**Advanced Workflows**

1. **Model Selection**

**\`/model\` - AI Model Management**

- **Purpose**: Interactive selection of AI models for conversations
- **Available Models**: Multiple Claude and other supported models
- **Use Cases**:
  - Choose optimal model for specific tasks
  - Switch models based on complexity requirements
  - Experiment with different AI capabilities

1. **Editor Integration**

**\`/editor\` - External Editor**

- **Purpose**: Open external editor (defaults to vi) for composing prompts
- **Use Cases**:
  - Write complex, multi-line prompts
  - Edit prompts with full editor capabilities
  - Compose detailed technical specifications

1. **Subscription Management**

**\`/subscribe\` - Pro Features**

- **Purpose**: Upgrade to Q Developer Pro for increased limits
- **Benefits**:
  - Higher query limits
  - Priority access
  - Advanced features

* * *

## **Best Practices**

### **Effective Agent Usage**

1. **Create Specialized Agents:** Design agents for specific workflows (AWS management, code review, documentation)
2. **Configure Tool Permissions:** Use allowedTools and toolsSettings for security
3. **Organize by Scope:** Use local agents for project-specific needs, global for general use
4. **Version Control:** Include agent configurations in project repositories

### **Context Management Strategies**

1. **Strategic Context Addition:** Add only relevant files to avoid token waste
2. **Use Knowledge Base:** Enable knowledge experiment for persistent context
3. **Regular Cleanup:** Use \`/compact\` to optimize long conversations
4. **Leverage Tangent Mode:** Keep help and exploration separate from main work

### **Security Considerations**

1. **Path Restrictions:** Configure allowedPaths and deniedPaths for file operations
2. **Command Filtering:** Use allowedCommands and deniedCommands for bash execution
3. **Service Limitations:** Restrict AWS services in use\_aws tool configuration
4. **Regular Audits:** Review tool permissions and agent configurations

### **Performance Optimization**

1. **Monitor Usage:** Use \`/usage\` to track context window utilization
2. **Compact Regularly:** Summarize long conversations to maintain performance
3. **Selective Context:** Add only necessary files to context
4. **Agent Switching:** Use appropriate agents for different tasks

* * *

## **Troubleshooting**

1. **Agent Not Loading**

- **Problem**: Agent configuration not recognized
- **Solutions**:
  - Verify JSON syntax in agent file
  - Check file location (.amazonq/cli-agents/ or ~/.aws/amazonq/cli-agents/)
  - Ensure proper permissions on agent files

**2\. Tool Permission Denied**

- **Problem**: Tools require permission for each use
- **Solutions**:
  - Configure allowedTools in agent configuration
  - Set up toolsSettings for automatic permissions
  - Use autoAllowReadonly for safe operations

**3\. Context Window Full**

- **Problem**: Conversation becomes too long
- **Solutions**:
  - Use \`/compact\` to summarize conversation
  - Start new conversation with \`/clear\`
  - Remove unnecessary context files

**4\. Experimental Features Not Working**

- **Problem**: Experimental features don't activate
- **Solutions**:
  - Enable through \`/experiment\` command
  - Check settings with \`q settings\`
  - Verify feature-specific configuration

**5\. Slow Response Times**

- **Solutions**:
  - Large context: Use \`/compact\` or reduce context files
  - Complex queries: Break into smaller, focused questions
  - Network issues: Check API timeout settings

* * *

## **Keyboard Shortcuts and Quick Access**

- _Ctrl+S:_ Fuzzy search through commands (configurable via \`chat.skimCommandKey\`)
- _Ctrl+T:_ Toggle tangent mode (configurable via \`chat.tangentModeKey\`)
- _Escape:_ Cancel current operation or exit menus
- _q login:_ to login into q cli
- _q logout:_ to logout from your q cli
- _q chat:_ start conversation with q cli

* * *

## **Integration Patterns**

### **MCP Server Integration**

- View loaded MCP servers: `/mcp`
- Configure MCP servers in agent

`{`

`"mcpServers": {`

`"git": {`

`"command": "mcp-git-server",`

`"args": ["--repo", "."]`

`}`

`}`

`}`

### **CI/CD Integration**

Q CLI can be integrated into continuous integration workflows:

- Non-interactive mode for scripts: `q chat --non-interactive "analyze this codebase"`
- Batch processing with saved conversations: `q chat load analysis-template`

* * *

## **Future Roadmap and Experimental Features**

### **Upcoming Features**

Based on the experimental system, Q CLI continues to evolve with:

- Enhanced knowledge base capabilities
- Improved agent system
- Better integration with development tools
- Advanced reasoning capabilities

### **Contributing to Development**

Q CLI is open source and welcomes contributions:

- Report issues through \`/issue\` command
- Contribute to GitHub repository
- Participate in feature discussions
- Test experimental features and provide feedback

* * *

## **Conclusion**

Amazon Q Developer CLI represents a significant advancement in AI-assisted development tools. Its comprehensive command system, flexible agent architecture, and powerful built-in tools make it an invaluable addition to any developer's toolkit.

Essential cookies cannot be deactivated, but you can choose “Customize” or “Decline” to decline performance cookies. If you agree, AWS and approved third parties will also use cookies to provide useful site features, remember your preferences, and display relevant content, including relevant advertising. To accept or decline all non-essential cookies, choose “Accept” or “Decline.” To make more detailed choices, choose “Customize.” AcceptDeclineCustomize ## Customize cookie preferences We use cookies and similar tools (collectively, "cookies") for the following purposes. ### Essential Essential cookies are necessary to provide our site and services and cannot be deactivated. They are usually set in response to your actions on the site, such as setting your privacy preferences, signing in, or filling in forms. ### Performance Performance cookies provide anonymous statistics about how customers navigate our site so we can improve site experience and performance. Approved third parties may perform analytics on our behalf, but they cannot use the data for their own purposes. Allowed ### Functional Functional cookies help us provide useful site features, remember your preferences, and display relevant content. with Amazon Q Developer, without administrator access. | July 9, 2024 | | Updated Amazon Q Developer admin policy | The [policy for empowering\
Amazon Q Developer administrators has been updated to include `sso:ListProfiles`.](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazonq/latest/qdeveloper-ug/q-admin-setup-admin-users.html) | June 19, 2024 | | Trusted access section | A [new section](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazonq/latest/qdeveloper-ug/q-admin-profile-sharing.html) more
clearly explains how a Amazon Q Developer administrator can share settings with member accounts. | June 19, 2024 | | Updated setup procedures | There's an improved [Getting started](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazonq/latest/aws-builder-use-ug/getting-started-q-dev.html)

examples](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazonq/latest/aws-builder-use-ug/inline-suggestions-code-examples.html) now include C and C++, and have improved examples for C#. | June 6, 2024 | | Updated managed policy: AmazonQFullAccess | Additional permissions have been added to the [AmazonQFullAccess](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazonq/latest/aws-builder-use-ug/managed-policy.html#amazonq-policy-fullaccess) policy. | April 30, 2024 | | New service-linked role: AWSServiceRoleForUserSubscriptions | The [AWSServiceRoleForUserSubscriptions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazonq/latest/aws-builder-use-ug/using-service-linked-roles-user-subs.html#service-linked-role-permissions-user-subs) service-linked role provides access for User

If you follow the Amazon Q product development, you may have heard the announcement of a new CLI agent called Amazon Q Developer CLI on Mar 6, 2025. The [Q CLI tool](https://github.com/aws/amazon-q-developer-cli) is now available as an open-source tool on Amazon Q Developer Free, and Pro tiers and in all [AWS regions](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazonq/latest/qdeveloper-ug/regions.html) where Q Developer is available. [Learn more](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/devops/introducing-the-enhanced-command-line-interface-in-amazon-q-developer/?trk=a59f8d3f-589a-44bf-8569-17c313bd4e50&sc_channel=el).

## Overview

> Amazon Q Developer CLI helps you efficiently complete tasks, improving and streamlining both the development and maintenance process without leaving the CLI environment.
>
> The Q CLI allows you to work with files on your local machine, query AWS resources, and write and test code. It can also help you debug issues through an interactive conversation.
>
> The Q CLI builds up its intelligence using context information you grant access including local document, project source code, your cloud account environment resources and tools access.

# Amazon Q Developer CLI announces custom agents

Posted on:
Jul 31, 2025


Amazon Q Developer CLI announces custom agents which enable you to customize the CLI agent to perform specialized tasks such as code reviews and troubleshooting more effectively. You can define a custom agent by creating a configuration file that specifies which tools an agent is allowed to use, prompts to guide its behavior, and the context needed to perform the task. Once configured, you can start a conversation in the CLI using the custom agent, reducing context switching and streamlining the experience.


During the set-up process, you can specify which specific MCP and native tools are available to the agent, including tool permissions, such as which paths the file system write tool can modify, providing you with more granular control of the agent’s behavior. You also have the ability to specify which files to statically include as context, and which files to include dynamically through context hooks, enabling more focused and tailored responses for your development tasks. Custom agents can be project specific and easily shared amongst your team members, or global for a developer to perform use-case based tasks across any project.


Custom agents are available within Amazon Q Developer CLI. To learn more, visit the Amazon Q Developer [documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazonq/latest/qdeveloper-ug/command-line-custom-agents.html) or read the [blog](https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/devops/overcome-development-disarray-with-amazon-q-developer-cli-custom-agents/).

setup, I'll quickly show you how the AM
identity center instance was created by
navigating to the console page for this
service. As you can see, my new user
account has been created. And when I
navigate to the application section,
you'll see the Q developer application
was created along with my user account
that has been associated with this
application.
Clicking on the setup link from the
email I received, I can now finish the
setup of my account by setting a new
password and logging in to show the
account setup was successful. For
security reasons, I'll skip over the MFA
setup
portion. Since this identity center
instance is an account instance and not
an organization instance, my user only
has access to the application it is
assigned to and not any other AWS
accounts, which is why you'll see me get
redirected to the Amazon Q product
page. Now that I've successfully
configured the Amazon Q developer
application and our user account, I'm
going to show you how we can install the
Amazon Q developer CLI. Since I am on a
Mac, I will use the installation
instructions for Mac OS, but please
follow the instructions for whichever
operating system you are using. The link
to the Amazon Q developer documentation
will be provided in the video
description for those following
along. For ease of use, I chose to
install the Q developer CLI through the
Home Brew package manager. To verify
that it was installed correctly, I'll
follow the instructions to check that I
get a result from querying the tool for
its
version. After successful installation,
I'll launch the Amazon Q developer
application which was installed to my
applications folder as part of the
installation.
Since we want to use the CLI with our
pro subscription, I'll navigate back to
the AM identity center console page and
copy the start URL that users of this
instance use to authenticate with
applications owned by the identity
center
instance. I'll likewise ensure that the
region matches the region where my
developer profile was created.
I'll also include a link in the
description to the supported regions
page for the Amazon Q developer
profile. Next, I'll allow access to the
application through the sign-in flow. To
ensure that the QLI launches with every
new shell session, some hooks can be
added to the scripts that are
automatically sourced when launching new
shell
sessions. If you'd prefer not to use the
provided standalone application, you can
likewise type Q login in your terminal
to initiate the login
process. Now that's it. You've
successfully set up Amazon Q developer
CLI with a pro subscription and can
start leveraging the power of Amazon Q
right from your terminal window. Q
developer can provide expert guidance
for building on AWS.
The CLI edition provides built-in
tooling for interacting with your shell
with the ability to create and read
files and navigate your file system.
Create feedback loops by running
applications and fixing error outputs
and is completely extensible with the
new addition of model context protocol
or MCP
support. Check the documentation for the
latest features and happy coding.
